ddls:
CREATE TABLE "bikes" (
  "bike_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bike model */,
  "bike_name" TEXT /* Name of the bike model */,
  "bike_type" TEXT /* Type of bike (e.g., road, mountain, hybrid) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the bike model */,
  "manufacturer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manufacturer */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when the bike model was released */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the bike model is currently active or discontinued */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bike_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sizes" (
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bike size */,
  "size_name" TEXT /* Name of the bike size (e.g., 46cm, 49cm) */,
  "size_value" REAL /* Value of the bike size */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the bike size (e.g., cm) */,
  "wheel_size" TEXT /* Wheel size (e.g., 26", 27.5", 29") */,
  "frame_material" TEXT /* Material used for the frame (e.g., carbon, aluminum) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., designer, engineer, product manager) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "geometry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the geometry specification being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the geometry data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the geometry data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the system */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device accessing the system */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Unique session ID for the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
